current.summary: Ran 5-agent panel discussion (3 rounds) on ACP trust anchor design, resolved all 5 open questions, applied decisions to acp-trust-anchor.md.

activeWork.description: Integrating trust anchor panel consensus into the ACP spec. Design doc (acp-trust-anchor.md) is updated with all panel decisions. Next: integrate changes into the actual spec (acp-spec.md).
activeWork.progress: 1) Set up 5-agent panel infrastructure (JSONL message bus, 5 personas). 2) Ran 3 rounds of panel discussion on 5 questions. 3) All 5 questions resolved with consensus (some with recorded dissents). 4) Applied all 7 changes to acp-trust-anchor.md: extraction algorithm fix (OID extension), Forgejo correction, publisher_identity field, sigstore_trust_root guidance, rollback WARNING, TOFU + repository_owner_id, sigstore-a2a reference. 5) Panel artifacts saved to docs/spec/acp/panel/ (message-bus.jsonl, consensus.md, personas.md).

recentDecisions: Q1: Add publisher_identity field — REQUIRED when signing identity differs from source_repo owner, absent for direct-publish. Verifiers MUST surface the distinction. (4-1, SPu recorded objection requiring companion verifier-behavior statement.)
Q2: Define sigstore_trust_root field in registry manifests. Public-good Sigstore as non-normative recommended default. (4-1, SPu dissent on defaults being tooling not protocol.)

Q3: Non-normative WARNING that source binding amplifies rollback risk + normative SHOULD for registries to maintain signed latest-version manifests. (4-1, SPu dissent on SHOULD without testable compliance criteria.)
Q4: Formally acknowledge TOFU for first-publish. Registries SHOULD implement first-publish policies. repository_owner_id as RECOMMENDED optional field. (5-0.)
Q5: Reference sigstore-a2a as related work, differentiate scope (agent cards vs content packages), no format coupling at v1. Coordinate on OID extension usage. (5-0.)
Extraction algorithm: Use Fulcio OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.57264.1.12 (Source Repository URI) instead of sub claim parsing. Simpler, more robust, handles subgroups.
Forgejo: Removed from provider tables — cannot sign via OIDC today (no Fulcio issuer support).
nextSteps: Read docs/spec/acp/ecosystem-landscape.md and take its context into account before modifying acp-spec.md — the ecosystem landscape may inform how trust anchor changes land in the spec.

Integrate trust anchor decisions into acp-spec.md: Section 2 (platform-first framing + trust chain narrative), Section 9.2 (add steps 5a-5b for source binding + publisher_identity verification), Section 11 (security considerations: rollback WARNING, TOFU, residual risks), new Appendix D (provider OIDC formats + sigstore-a2a reference).
Add new meta.yaml fields to the spec schema: publisher_identity, repository_owner_id, sigstore_trust_root (in registry manifests).
Write intro rewrite for acp-trust-anchor.md (deferred until after panel — panel is now complete).

context.notes:
The panel ran as 5 independent Sonnet agents with a shared JSONL message bus (docs/spec/acp/panel/message-bus.jsonl). 3 rounds, 18 total bus entries. Full consensus doc at docs/spec/acp/panel/consensus.md.

The Spec Purist had 3 recorded dissents — all were 'can live with it but want objection on record.' These are the spec-drafting pressure points to get right: (1) publisher_identity must have companion verifier-behavior normative text, (2) sigstore_trust_root default must be non-normative not normative, (3) rollback SHOULD should have testable criteria or be downgraded to non-normative.

The acp-trust-anchor.md doc is now the authoritative design record with all panel decisions applied. The acp-spec.md needs to be updated to match — that's the next session's primary task.

IMPORTANT: Read docs/spec/acp/ecosystem-landscape.md before modifying the spec. Holden specifically asked for this to be taken into account — the ecosystem landscape document may contain context about how ACP relates to other specs and projects that should inform where and how the trust anchor changes land.

previousSessions.summary: Deep research on ACP trust anchor design; ready to run multi-agent panel discussion on 5 open questions.
completedWork: Walked through 7 brainstorming questions with Holden (2 decided, 4 sent to panel, 1 deferred)
Ran 4 deep research agents (OIDC formats, SSH APIs, Sigstore federation, supply chain trust models)
Research revealed 3 critical findings: extraction algorithm must use OID not sub, Forgejo can't sign, npm already solved this
nextSteps: Architect 5-agent panel with shared JSONL message bus
After panel: synthesize consensus, update docs
